summary: This notebook demonstrates a dual-path inference pipeline for a text classification competition, combining a large language model with a retrieval-augmented semantic search approach. It first generates predictions using a quantized Qwen2.5 32B model via vLLM with a logits processor for binary classification. Second, it leverages a fine-tuned Qwen3 0.6B embedding model to perform dense retrieval against a constructed corpus of positive and negative examples, computing rule-violation scores via semantic similarity. The two prediction streams are combined through rank-based blending to produce the final submission. Readers learn how to efficiently deploy quantized LLMs for inference, adapt embedding models for retrieval-augmented classification, and effectively fuse heterogeneous model outputs.
competition_flow: Loads test data, generates predictions via a quantized Qwen2.5 32B LLM and a Qwen3 0.6B embedding-based semantic search, then blends the ranked outputs to produce a final submission CSV.
data_reading: Reads test.csv and train.csv from Kaggle input directories using pandas.read_csv.
data_processing: Constructs prompts by formatting subreddit, rule, and few-shot examples into a structured text template.
Cleans text using the cleantext library (fixing unicode, removing URLs/emails/phones, preserving punctuation/numbers).
Maps binary labels to 1/-1 for the embedding scoring step.
Normalizes embeddings and applies softmax to LLM logprobs for probability calibration.
ensembling: Combines two submission files by ranking their predicted probabilities, normalizing ranks to [0,1], and averaging them with equal weights (0.5 each) before saving the final submission.
insights: Quantizing a 32B LLM to AWQ INT4 enables feasible inference on available GPU memory while maintaining performance.
Semantic search with a fine-tuned embedding model can effectively leverage few-shot examples as a corpus for rule-based classification.
Rank-based blending of heterogeneous model outputs often yields more robust predictions than raw probability averaging.
